headline: Nasdaq Dips on Hawkish Fed, Chip Sell-Off; Netflix Forecast Disappoints
summary: US equities saw mixed performance with the Nasdaq down sharply amid rising Treasury yields and a broad chip-stock slump, despite strong earnings from TSMC. Netflix shares tumbled after a disappointing growth forecast, while several AI-related firms announced IPO plans and robust guidance. Hawkish Fedspeak contributed to market caution.

bullets.lead: AI chip stocks experienced a broad sell-off despite strong TSMC earnings.

segments.text: The Nasdaq fell sharply, driven by a slump in chip stocks and rising Treasury yields, overshadowing TSMC's record performance.

sources.title: 미국증시, TSMC 호실적에도 AI 반도체 매도…국채금리 부담에 나스닥 1....
Nasdaq Falls Sharply as Chip-Stock Slump Overshadows TSMC Record
Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Manpower, Abbott, UnitedHealth, TSMC & more

segments.text: Micron Technology shares dropped 5.6% following reports of infrastructure cost shocks from TSMC and ASML.

sources.title: 마이크론, TSMC·ASML 인프라 비용 쇼크에 주가 5.6% 급락

bullets.lead: Google is exploring Samsung for future AI chip production.

segments.text: Google is considering Samsung Electronics for a portion of its next-generation AI chip, the 10th-gen TPU 'Icefish', specifically for the 2nm I/O die, due to TSMC's tight production capacity and Samsung's staff shortage from surging 2nm foundry demand.
This move could significantly strengthen Samsung's AI chip foundry references, following Tesla AI6 and Nvidia's next-gen LPU.

bullets.lead: Global semiconductor equipment sales forecasts are rising on AI demand.

segments.text: SEMI has increased its global semiconductor equipment sales projections to $165.9 billion by 2026, primarily driven by robust AI-related demand for leading-edge technologies.
Japan is aggressively acquiring 27,500 Nvidia Rubin chips to advance its robotics and AI sectors.

sources.title: 日本狂掃27,500顆輝達Rubin 拚發展機器人、AI

bullets.lead: TSMC is pushing back on ASML's equipment price increases.

segments.text: TSMC is reportedly resisting ASML's plans to raise equipment prices, although ASML intends to proceed with the increases.

bullets.lead: Netflix shares tumbled on a disappointing growth forecast.

segments.text: Despite reporting a 13% revenue increase to $12.6 billion, Netflix's shares fell as its growth forecast disappointed investors, with the company expecting a slowdown.

sources.title: Netflix Revenue Grows 13% to $12.6 Billion
Netflix earnings forecast disappoints Wall Street, shares tumble
Netflix Revenue and Profit Increase, but the Company Expects Growth to Slow
Netflix Reports Slowing Revenue Growth for Second Quarter
Netflix earnings forecast disappoints investors, shares tumble
Netflix Pledges More Programs, AI to Reverse Slowing Growth
Netflix shares slide on disappointing growth forecasts

segments.text: The company pledged more programs and AI integration to reverse slowing growth.

sources.title: Netflix Pledges More Programs, AI to Reverse Slowing Growth

bullets.lead: AEHR Test Systems reported strong Q4 earnings and robust FY2027 guidance.

segments.text: AEHR Test Systems announced strong Q4 earnings and an impressive FY2027 revenue guidance of $130-$150 million, driven by accelerating demand for wafer-level burn-in from AI process.

bullets.lead: ASML delivered stronger-than-expected quarterly results and raised guidance.

segments.text: ASML reported robust quarterly performance and significantly boosted its full-year net sales guidance to "43-45 billion, fueled by strong demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment.

bullets.lead: Treasury yields rose on hawkish Fedspeak.

segments.text: Treasury yields increased as Federal Reserve officials delivered hawkish remarks, with Dallas Fed President Logan calling for 'modestly' higher interest rates.

sources.title: Treasury Yields Rise as Fedspeak Leans Hawkish
Dallas Fed President Logan calls for 'modestly' higher interest rates

segments.text: New York Fed President Williams, however, stated that inflation has peaked and rates are 'well positioned'.

sources.title: New York Fed President Williams says inflation has peaked, rates 'well positioned'

bullets.lead: Market sentiment was impacted by rising rates and inflation concerns.

segments.text: The Nasdaq saw a significant decline, partly attributed to the burden of rising national debt interest rates.

bullets.lead: Trump's teleprompter operator is under scrutiny for betting on speeches.

segments.text: The White House teleprompter operator was placed on unpaid leave following reports that he made over $100,000 betting on Trump's speeches on prediction markets.

sources.title: White House teleprompter operator on unpaid leave after reports he made more than $100k betting on Trump's speeches
White House teleprompter operator accused of making $100k off Trump speech bets

bullets.lead: The US government is reportedly seeking to join Trump's defamation suit against the BBC.

segments.text: The US government is reportedly looking to join Trump's $10 billion defamation lawsuit against the BBC.

sources.title: U.S. Government Reportedly Wants To Join Trump’s $10 Billion Defamation Suit Against BBC

bullets.lead: Trump imposed 25% tariffs on Brazilian goods.

segments.text: President Trump implemented 25% tariffs on goods from Brazil.

sources.title: Trump imposes 25% tariffs on Brazilian goods

segments.text: Senator Elizabeth Warren criticized Trump's CFPB overhaul, claiming it has cost Americans $26.5 billion.

sources.title: Sen. Warren says Trump's CFPB overhaul has cost Americans $26.5 billion

bullets.lead: AI companies are pursuing IPOs and open-sourcing initiatives.

segments.text: DeepSeek is raising $7.4 billion at a $71 billion pre-money valuation and is preparing for an IPO on Shanghai’s STAR Market as early as next year.
Anthropic is reportedly planning investor meetings for an IPO as early as October, following a $65 billion financing round.
Elon Musk announced that Grok Build is now open source, with Grok 4.5 continuously improving.

bullets.lead: Microsoft is developing an AI bug finder.

segments.text: Microsoft is reportedly preparing a Mythos-like AI bug finder.

sources.title: Exclusive: Microsoft Preps Mythos-Like AI Bug Finder

bullets.lead: DRAM price increases are facing customer pushback.

segments.text: Customers are resisting 30% DRAM price increases, leading to a lowered Q3 DRAM price growth forecast, while NAND demand remains strong due to KV cache offloading and substitution.

bullets.lead: Samsung is investing heavily in advanced memory production.

segments.text: Samsung Electronics is nearing over KRW 10 trillion in semiconductor equipment orders for its Pyeongtaek Plant 5 (P5), which will produce cutting-edge DRAM and HBM.

x_themes.topic: AEHR's Strong Earnings and AI/Optical/Memory Testing Outlook
x_themes.summary: Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) reported strong Q4 earnings and an impressive FY2027 revenue guidance of $130-$150M, driven by accelerating demand for wafer-level burn-in from AI processors, silicon photonics, and new opportunities in HBM/NAND memory testing.

x_themes.topic: ASML Strong Earnings, Raised Guidance, and Capacity Expansion
x_themes.summary: ASML reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and significantly boosted its full-year net sales guidance to €43-45 billion, driven by strong demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and plans to increase both low NA EUV and DUV immersion capacity by 30% for 2027 and potentially 2028.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Q2 Earnings Expectations and AI Outlook
x_themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 earnings are expected to show a 59% net profit increase and record gross/operating margins, with investors keenly awaiting updates on AI demand health, CoWoS capacity, N2/N3 ramp, and potential price increases, alongside a raised 2024-2029 AI semiconductor revenue CAGR forecast of 70-80%.

Samsung Electronics is nearing over KRW 10 trillion in semiconductor equipment orders for its Pyeongtaek Plant 5 (P5), which will produce cutting-edge DRAM and HBM, with Phase 1 potentially operational by next year to meet surging memory demand.

x_themes.topic: Memory Market Dynamics: DRAM Price Pushback & Strong NAND Demand
x_themes.summary: Customers are pushing back against 30% DRAM price increases, leading to a lowered Q3 DRAM price growth forecast, while NAND demand is strong due to KV cache offloading and substitution for expensive DRAM.

x_themes.topic: Samsung Considers Outsourcing Google 2nm TPU I/O Die Design
x_themes.summary: Samsung Electronics is considering outsourcing the back-end design for Google's 2nm TPU I/O die due to a staff shortage from surging 2nm foundry demand, with Google's compute processor to be made by TSMC at 1.4nm and the I/O die by Samsung at 2nm.

x_themes.topic: SEMI Raises Global Semiconductor Equipment Sales Forecasts
x_themes.summary: SEMI has raised its global semiconductor equipment sales forecasts for the next three years, projecting $165.9 billion in 2026, driven by strong AI-related demand for leading-edge logic, memory chips, and advanced packaging.

x_themes.topic: PJM Capacity Auction Reveals Structural Power Shortfall
x_themes.summary: The 2028/2029 PJM capacity auction confirmed a persistent structural shortfall in dispatchable and reliably accredited capacity, with the clearing price hitting an administratively imposed ceiling of $325/MW-day despite an estimated unconstrained price of $554.72/MW-day, highlighting long-term scarcity.

DeepSeek is raising $7.4 billion at a $71 billion pre-money valuation with nearly $500 million ARR, and is preparing for an IPO on Shanghai’s STAR Market as early as next year, while also seeking US dollar-denominated capital from overseas investors.
Anthropic is reportedly planning investor meetings in the coming weeks for an IPO as early as October, following a $65 billion financing round at a $965 billion valuation last May.

Nvidia has introduced new Thor robotics computer modules, the T3000 and T2000, which are half the size and power of previous versions while delivering similar AI inference performance, with shipments scheduled for Q1 2027.

x_themes.topic: Apple's Potential AI Chip Acquisitions and Capex Increase
x_themes.summary: Apple is reportedly looking to acquire AI chip companies to bolster its own AI infrastructure, potentially leading to a significant increase in its AI capex spend, which could be bullish for related sectors like optical networking and foundries.
Elon Musk announced that Grok Build is now open source, with Grok 4.5 continuously improving, contributing to the growing landscape of US-based open-source large language models.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Pushing Back on ASML Equipment Pricing
x_themes.summary: TSMC is reportedly pushing back against ASML's plans to raise equipment prices, although ASML intends to proceed with the price increases.
